task_completion.description: Create the first 10mm-diameter cylinder centered at the origin and extruded symmetrically by at least 15mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D cylinder is visible in the SolveSpace viewport. A 2D sketch circle should have a diameter constraint of 10mm (often visible as 'd=10' or similar), and the extrusion property should show a symmetric extrusion with a distance of 15mm or greater.

task_completion.description: Create a second 10mm-diameter cylinder orthogonal to the first, centered at the origin, and extruded symmetrically by at least 15mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second cylinder intersecting the first at a 90-degree angle is visible. It should also have a 10mm diameter constraint and an extrusion distance of at least 15mm.

task_completion.description: Use a Boolean intersection to retain only the overlapping volume of the two cylinders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Property Browser/Group window shows the second group is set to 'Intersection' (or equivalent Boolean operation). The 3D viewport displays a Steinmetz solid (a shape with curved faces meeting at sharp edges, resembling a rounded cube or a tent-like structure), and the non-overlapping parts of the cylinders are removed.
The SolveSpace window title bar displays 'fluid_core.slvs' or a save dialog is visible showing the file being saved to the '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory with the name 'fluid_core.slvs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to model the shape.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace application window with active toolbars, property browser, and 3D viewport, rather than a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the 3D modeling tools to build the shape.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a logical progression of steps (e.g., drawing a sketch, applying constraints, extruding, creating a new group) rather than just loading a pre-made file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI modeling process.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run external scripts that automatically generate the .slvs file; all modeling actions occur within the SolveSpace UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target shape is a Steinmetz solid (the intersection of two orthogonal cylinders). The expected volume is exactly 666.67 mm³ (tolerance 80.0 mm³). The expected bounding box dimensions are 10.0 mm along each axis (tolerance 0.5 mm). Visually, the final shape should fit perfectly inside a 10x10x10 mm cube, appearing as a rounded, pillowy cube with sharp intersecting edges.
